criterion_text: Undergoing endoscopic treatment of pancreatic fluid collections
Age ≥ 18 years
Functional hyposplenism or asplenia, patient under long-term antibiotic prophylaxis for this reason
Choledocholithiasis not resolved by ERCP, post-ERCP perforation, and post-ERCP concomitant pancreatitis
Providing signed informed consent
Non-pregnant
Blood routine: the total number of white blood cells < 3.0×10^9 or neutrophils < 1.5× 10^9
Ill-controlled hypertension: diastolic blood pressure≥100mmHg, systolic blood pressure ≥160 mmHg

Calcineurin inhibitor-resistant after ≥6 months of adequate dosing, OR calcineurin inhibitor-intolerant with documented contraindications, OR inadequate response or disease relapse after treatment with ≥2 other immunosuppressive agents
Persistent urinary protein-to-creatinine (UP/C) ratio >1.0 g/g
Steroid-dependence or frequently relapsing disease, unless complete remission achieved
eGFR <60 mL/min/1.73 m² using the modified Bedside Schwartz formula
Age ≥ 18 years
Muscle invasive bladder cancer that is cisplatin-ineligible or declined based on Galsky criteria
Age under 18 years old
Histology of pure adenocarcinoma, pure squamous cell carcinoma, or pure small cell carcinoma in the TURBT sample
Documented medical history of migraine with or without aura for at least 6 months before the Screening Visit
Age ≥18
Continuous migraine (defined as an unrelenting headache) within 1 month prior to Screening Visit
Significant cardiovascular disease (unstable angina, severe arrhythmias, recent MI)
Guardian and subject (if of independent informed age) willing and able to provide written informed consent to participate in the study
Patients who intend to take isavuconazole for the treatment of invasive mycosis
History of radiotherapy within 12 months prior to enrollment
No diagnosis of probable or proven invasive pulmonary infection with Mucorales according to the 2020 EORTC/MSGERC criteria
Male and female children
Minor or adult patient ≥ 4 years of age
Oncologic patients at risk of neoplastic cachexia
lack of informed consent
Patient able to understand and sign a written informed consent form, or written informed consent form signed by the patients' legally authorized representative
Diagnosis of RA using the 2010 American College of Rheumatology (ACR) criteria
Active hepatitis B (positive hepatitis B surface antigen and HBV DNA >upper limit of normal) or active hepatitis C (positive hepatitis C antibody and HCV RNA >upper limit of normal)
Inability to give informed consent
Age 18 years and above
Detection of elevated levels of EBV DNA, RNA or proteins in affected tissues OR elevated levels of EBV DNA in the peripheral blood
Any other condition that, in the opinion of the investigator, would make the patient unsuitable for enrollment or could interfere with the patient participating in the study
Treatment with immunosuppressive medication including ≥10mg prednisone equivalent
Present to the hospital with a visual acuity of hand motion or better in the study eye
Planned combined phacovitrectomy procedure at one of the selected surgical sites
Scleral buckling is indicated
History of uncontrolled hypertension
